data.title: iExec RLC Partners With IncentiveFi
data.description:
iExec has partnered with IncentiveFi to introduce a marketplace that connects crypto theft victims with investors. The system enables secure, private alerts on recovery claims, allowing victims to regain liquidity while investors can profit from successful recoveries. This initiative aims to enhance transparency and efficiency in handling stolen digital assets.

RLC Info

iExec is a decentralized cloud computing platform based on the Ethereum blockchain. It provides on-demand computing resources for decentralized applications (dApps) on its cloud. Unlike traditional cloud services, iExec offers a blockchain network that integrates various computing resources, facilitating the smooth operation of dApps by distributing their computational processes.

iExec ensures efficient dApp functioning by offloading processing-intensive computations off-chain, maintaining the uninterrupted operation of on-chain blockchain activities. This decentralization of computations is achieved using XtremWeb-HEP, an open-source Desktop Grid Software. This software facilitates Desktop Grid computing, which leverages unused computing power from various sources for dApp processing. With XtremWeb-HEP, iExec can provide a vast array of essential features, like fault-tolerance, multi-application support, and hybrid public/private infrastructure, allowing dApps to access any computing resource within the iExec framework.

The RLC token is iExec’s native cryptocurrency. It serves as the primary medium to access the services within the iExec infrastructure. This means that to utilize iExec’s decentralized computing resources or other platform-related services, users would primarily engage through the RLC token.

data.title: LENNAR CORP /NEW/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lennar Corp, a leading home construction and real estate company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped its recent activities.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $8,810 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $9,416 million, primarily due to lower homebuilding revenues.

Net Earnings Attributable to Lennar: $591 million, a significant decrease from $1,163 million in the prior year, impacted by reduced revenues and increased costs.

Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Share: $2.29, down from $4.26 in the previous year, reflecting the decline in net earnings.

Earnings Before Income Taxes: $791 million, compared to $1,535 million in the previous year, indicating a decrease in profitability.

Provision for Income Taxes: $191 million, with an effective tax rate of 24.4%, slightly higher than the previous year's 23.0%.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Homebuilding segment reported revenues of $8,253,675 for the three months ended August 31, 2025, with the East, Central, South Central, and West regions contributing significantly. The Financial Services segment generated $314,195, while the Multifamily segment reported $228,465 in revenues.

Geographical Performance: The East region experienced a decline in revenues to $1,703,457 from $2,074,799 in the previous year, while the Central region saw a slight decrease to $2,075,062. The South Central region, however, showed growth with revenues increasing to $1,508,269 from $1,286,625.

Sales Units: The Homebuilding segment's performance was driven by the sale of single-family attached and detached homes, with a focus on first-time, move-up, and active adult homebuyers.

New Product Launches: The acquisition of Rausch Coleman Homes expanded the company's footprint into new markets, including Arkansas, Oklahoma, Alabama, and Kansas/Missouri, enhancing its product offerings in these regions.

New Production Launches: The company completed the spin-off of Millrose Properties, Inc., contributing $5.6 billion in land assets, which aligns with its strategy to become a pure-play, asset-light, new home manufacturing company.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued variability in quarterly results due to seasonality and market conditions. The adoption of new accounting standards and strategic acquisitions are expected to influence future operational performance.

SEC Filing: LENNAR CORP /NEW/ [ LEN ] - 10-Q - Oct. 03, 2025

data.title: Heard on the Street Recap: Farmer's Market — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

The White House may be considering an extension of Obamacare subsidies. Trump aides are discussing proposals as advisers worry that the Republican Party will take blame for raising healthcare costs for millions of Americans.

President Trump is considering $10 billion-plus in aid to American farmers. The trade war with China has hurt soybean farmers as China reduced its imports of soybeans from America. The aid would be potentially funded with tariff revenue.

The Friday government jobs report was postponed. The Bureau of Labor Statistics isn't issuing reports because of the government shutdown. Alternative sources could help fill the gap.

The Goldman Sachs CEO said he expects a stock-market "drawdown" in the next year or two. David Solomon said at an event that markets tend to run ahead of the potential whenever there is a significant acceleration in new technology.

Stocks were mixed. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.5%, or 239 points. The S&P 500 was flat, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.3%.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Stratis to Reveal Logo
data.description:
Stratis is set to unveil its new logo on October 3rd.

Refer to the official tweet by STRAX:

Stratisplatform
@stratisplatform

The wait is almost over.

The new Stratis logo drops tomorrow. 🚀 pic.twitter.com/eZU77awWbk

Oct 02, 2025

STRAX Info

Stratis is a powerful and flexible blockchain development platform designed for the needs of real-world enterprise and financial services. Launched in 2016 by Chris Trew, Stratis provides businesses and organizations the tools to test and deploy their own private blockchains within the native Stratis ecosystem. The key differentiator of Stratis is that it allows developers to build applications in the widely understood language of C# .NET, a factor which aims to increase adoption and ease of use.

The platform’s native cryptocurrency token, STRAX, is used within its ecosystem to facilitate various operations and secure the network. Stratis also offers a proof-of-stake (PoS) consensus algorithm, meaning holders of STRAX can stake their coins in return for transaction verification rewards. It has a unique feature called TumbleBit, which provides privacy for users by making transactions nearly impossible to trace. In a nutshell, Stratis seeks to offer an easy-to-use platform for developers and businesses wanting to create custom blockchain solutions.

data.title: VenHub Global Signed a SPAC Deal in Late 2024 That Was Terminated in 2025
Tianci International, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Tianci International, Inc., a global logistics service provider through its subsidiary Roshing International Co., Limited, has released its annual 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. The company, which primarily operates in Hong Kong and the Asia-Pacific region, has shown significant developments in its logistics services and strategic expansions.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Operating Revenues: $9.28 million, an 8% increase from the previous year, driven by an expanding customer base, particularly high-paying customers.
  • Gross Profit: $0.45 million, a 57% decrease due to higher logistics costs outpacing revenue growth.
  • (Loss) Income from Operations: $(2.71) million, a significant decline from the previous year's income of $0.17 million, primarily due to increased general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income (loss): $(2.69) million, compared to a net income of $0.11 million in the previous year, reflecting increased operating expenses and stable service pricing amidst rising costs.
  • Net (loss) income attributable to TIANCI INTERNATIONAL, INC.: $(2.64) million, a substantial decrease from the previous year's net income of $0.05 million, highlighting the impact of increased expenses and strategic investments.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Tianci International, Inc., through its subsidiary Roshing International Co., Limited, provides global logistics services, focusing on ocean freight forwarding, including container and bulk goods shipping. The company operates primarily in Hong Kong and the Asia-Pacific region, with strategic extensions to other continents.
  • Revenue Segments: The logistics service revenue represented 97% of the company's total revenue for the years ended July 31, 2025, and 2024, indicating a strong focus on logistics as the primary business segment.
  • Geographical Performance: Roshing's logistics services are primarily carried out in Hong Kong and other locations in the Asia-Pacific region, mainly in Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company also ships goods to African countries, indicating a diverse geographical reach.
  • New Product Launches: The company is launching a global mineral trade business, accumulating a high-grade inventory of industrial metals, and plans to integrate ore distribution with existing shipping operations to provide end-to-end supply chain solutions for metallurgical and steelmaking customers.
  • Operational Strategy: Roshing adopts an asset-light strategy, leveraging senior management's expertise in the global logistics industry to drive significant growth in logistics revenue since 2023.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand its global logistics network to South America and Africa, develop a proprietary presence in the global market for bulk chrome and manganese ore, and prioritize environmentally friendly practices to reduce carbon emissions.
  • Management Expertise: The CEO, Shufang Gao, brings over 20 years of management experience in shipping operation management and logistics transportation, providing a managerial framework for expanding the global logistics business.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Expansion: Tianci International, Inc. has strategically expanded its business operations by acquiring RQS United Group Limited, which holds a 90% stake in Roshing International Co., Limited. This acquisition is aimed at enhancing the company's global logistics services.
  • New Market Entry: The company is preparing to enter the global trade of bulk chrome and manganese ore, which is expected to create synergies with its existing logistics business.
  • Capital Management: The company successfully completed a $7 million initial public offering on Nasdaq, significantly bolstering its capital resources. The proceeds were used to enhance liquidity and working capital, with net proceeds amounting to approximately $5.44 million after deducting offering expenses. Tianci International, Inc. has not declared any dividends, as it intends to reinvest earnings into business development and expansion. The company also issued 87,500 warrants to a consultant as part of its strategic advisory services.
  • Future Outlook: Tianci International, Inc. anticipates that its liquidity and working capital will be sufficient to sustain operations for the next twelve months. The company is focused on expanding its logistics services and entering the mineral ore trading market, which is expected to become a new profit driver. The company plans to leverage its public listing to explore further investment opportunities and enhance shareholder value.

Challenges and Risks

  • Geopolitical Risks: The company faces significant geopolitical risks, particularly due to political instability, conflicts, and international hostilities affecting the maritime transportation industry. This could adversely impact operations conducted outside the United States, especially in regions with ongoing tensions such as the United States and China, Russia, Ukraine, and the European Union.
  • Trade Protectionism: Trade protectionism poses a material risk, potentially leading to increased costs, reduced global trade, and adverse impacts on the company's charterers' business, affecting their ability to make timely payments and renew contracts.
  • Competitive Landscape: The logistics industry is highly competitive, with intense price and service quality competition. Larger competitors with more resources may pursue acquisitions and technological advancements, challenging the company's market position.
  • Climate Change: Climate change and related regulatory measures could disrupt operations, increase compliance costs, and shift customer demands, impacting financial results.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include potential accidents, cargo damage, and transportation-related incidents, which could result in significant financial costs and reputational damage.
  • Customer Needs: The company is also exposed to risks from changing customer logistics needs and contractual obligations, which could lead to financial losses and damage to its reputation if not managed proactively.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect financial results. Additionally, the potential for increased regulatory scrutiny and changes in international trade policies could impact operations and profitability.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by fluctuating demand and supply for cargo space and container loads, which could lead to financial volatility. The company must manage these fluctuations to maintain stable revenues and operating income. The company is also addressing the impact of rising inflation and interest rates, which have increased labor and borrowing costs. Management is implementing efficiency optimization initiatives and considering modest rate increases to mitigate these pressures.

SEC Filing: Tianci International, Inc. [ CIIT ] - 10-K - Oct. 03, 2025

data.title: Trump's $100,000 fee for H-1B worker visas challenged in lawsuit
data.description:
By Daniel Wiessner

A coalition of unions, employers and religious groups filed a lawsuit on Friday seeking to block President Donald Trump's bid to impose a $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for high-skilled foreign workers.

The lawsuit filed in federal court in San Francisco is the first to challenge Trump's proclamation issued last month announcing the fee.

The United Auto Workers union, American Association of University Professors and other plaintiffs say Trump's power to restrict the entry of certain foreign nationals does not allow him to override the law that created the H-1B visa program.

The program allows U.S. employers to hire foreign workers in specialty fields, and technology companies in particular rely heavily on workers who receive H-1B visas.

Critics of H-1Bs and other work visa programs say they are often used to replace American workers with cheaper foreign labor. But business groups and major companies have said H-1Bs are a critical means to address a shortage of qualified American workers.

Employers who sponsor H-1B workers currently typically pay between $2,000 and $5,000 in fees, depending on the size of the company and other factors.

Trump's order bars new H-1B recipients from entering the United States unless the employer sponsoring their visa has made an additional $100,000 payment. The administration has said the order does not apply to people who already hold H-1B visas or those who submitted applications before September 21.

data.title: THORChain to Hold AMA on X on October 4th
data.description:
THORChain will host an AMA on X on October 4th at 15:00 UTC.

Refer to the official tweet by RUNE:

THORChain
@THORChain

Mark your calendars, Chads â°🔥@KentonC137 & @patriotsounds are back with a new episode of Chad's Lounge featuring the OG @CBarraford.

👉 This Saturday, 3PM UTC. Donâ€™t miss it 🗓ï¸https://t.co/Gxwt2kXiO2

Oct 02, 2025

RUNE Info

THORChain is a decentralized liquidity protocol that enables users to easily exchange cryptocurrency tokens over the network without the need for intermediaries. This is achieved by creating a liquidity pool that automatically establishes prices and provides liquidity for trading.

The key feature of THORChain is its ability to facilitate secure, fast, and reliable cross-chain asset operations. This means that users can directly exchange one cryptocurrency for another, even if they exist on different blockchains.

The RUNE token in THORChain is used as collateral for validators in the network and to ensure liquidity in the liquidity pools.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum Files 8K - Regulation FD >OXY
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum Corp. (OXY) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on October 03, 2025.

On October 2, 2025, Occidental issued a press release with respect to the management change described above. The press release, furnished as Exhibit 99.1 hereto, shall not be deemed "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, nor shall it be deemed incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/797468/000114036125037245/ef20056571_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/797468/000114036125037245/0001140361-25-037245-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >OXY
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum Corp. (OXY) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on October 03, 2025.

On October 1, 2025, the Board of Directors (the "Board") of Occidental Petroleum Corporation ("Occidental" or the "Company") promoted Richard A. Jackson, 49, to Chief Operating Officer, effective immediately. He will continue serving as Senior Vice President, a position to which he was appointed in October 2020.

Mr. Jackson has over 25 years of experience in the oil and gas industry, holding a variety of technical and leadership roles. Mr. Jackson has been an employee of Occidental since 2003, and most recently served as President of Operations for U.S. Onshore Resources and Carbon Management since October 2020. Prior to that, Mr. Jackson served as President and General Manager, EOR and Oxy Low Carbon Ventures, LLC; President Low Carbon Ventures; Senior Vice President, Operation Support; Vice President, Investor Relations; and President and General Manager Permian Resources Delaware Basin. Mr. Jackson holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Petroleum Engineering from Texas A&M University.

In connection with his promotion, Mr. Jackson's base salary was increased to $925,000 per year, and he is eligible for a target annual cash incentive award of $925,000 for 2025, each effective on or about October 1, 2025. Mr. Jackson also received a grant of restricted stock units valued at $1,500,000, which vests in equal annual installments over a three-year period subject to Mr. Jackson's continued service through the applicable vesting dates.

Mr. Jackson does not have any family relationships with any director, executive officer or person nominated or chosen to become a director or executive officer of the Company, and there are no arrangements or understandings between him and any other person pursuant to which Mr. Jackson was appointed as an officer of the Company. There are no related party transactions involving Mr. Jackson that are reportable under Item 404(a) of Regulation S-K.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/797468/000114036125037245/ef20056571_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/797468/000114036125037245/0001140361-25-037245-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: DJIA Rises 1.10% This Week to 46758.28 — Data Talk
data.description:
The Dow Jones Industrial Average is up 510.99 points or 1.10% this week to 46758.28

  • Largest one-week point and percentage gain since the week ending Aug 22, 2025
  • Up three of the past four weeks
  • Today it is up 238.56 points or 0.51%
  • A new record close
  • 11th record close of 2025
  • Largest one-day point and percentage gain since Friday, Sept. 26, 2025
  • Up for six consecutive trading days
  • Up 810.96 points or 1.76% over the last six trading days
  • Largest six-day point and percentage gain since Thursday, Sept. 11, 2025
  • Longest winning streak since Friday, May 2, 2025 when the market rose for nine straight trading days
  • Up 10.74% from the Election Day close of 42221.88 on Tuesday, Nov. 5, 2024
  • Up 7.52% from the Inauguration Day close of 43487.83 on Monday, Jan 20, 2025
  • Up 10.74% since tariffs were announced on Wednesday, April 2, 2025
  • Up 24.21% from its 52-week low of 37645.59 hit Tuesday, April 8, 2025
  • Rose 10.40% from 52 weeks ago
  • Up 24.21% from its 2025 closing low of 37645.59 hit Tuesday, April 8, 2025
  • Year-to-date it is up 4214.06 points or 9.91%

Data based on preliminary market closing values

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet
